item.title: Alfreda Fisher shines as Drua prepare for first home clash
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Women's Drua loose forward Alfreda Fisher has emerged as the leading tackler in the Super Rugby Women's Competition following her outstanding performance against the Waratahs in round one. The Navy officer delivered an impressive all-round display, making a significant impact across the field during her 73-minute stint and proving instrumental to the Drua's efforts.
item.content:encoded:
McDonald's Fiji Women's Drua loose forward Alfreda Fisher has emerged as the leading tackler in the Super Rugby Women's Competition following her outstanding performance against the Waratahs in round one.

The Navy officer delivered an impressive all-round display, making a significant impact across the field during her 73-minute stint and proving instrumental to the Drua's efforts.

Hailing from Taveuni, Fisher showcased the experience and quality she has gained through numerous international appearances for the Fijiana national team in both the 15s and 7s formats.

The Drua players are calling on fans to come out in support ahead of this weekend's clash against the Western Force at Churchill Park in Lautoka at 2pm Saturday.

item.title: Bula Boys held to 2-all braw by Vanuatu in Friendly
item.description: Bula Boys held to 2-all braw by Vanuatu in Friendly

item.title: Neymar on track for World Cup return after positive injury update
item.description: Neymar’s hopes of featuring at the FIFA World Cup 2026 have been boosted after Brazil confirmed the forward is making “good progress” in his recovery from a calf injury. The Brazilian Football Confederation says a recent scan showed encouraging signs, with the 34-year-old continuing his recovery under the national team’s medical staff.
item.content:encoded:
Neymar’s hopes of featuring at the FIFA World Cup 2026 have been boosted after Brazil confirmed the forward is making “good progress” in his recovery from a calf injury.

The Brazilian Football Confederation says a recent scan showed encouraging signs, with the 34-year-old continuing his recovery under the national team’s medical staff.

Neymar was initially ruled out for two to three weeks after suffering a grade-two muscle tear last month and has already missed Brazil’s recent warm-up matches against Panama and Egypt.

The former Barcelona and Paris Saint-Germain star, who has scored 79 goals in 128 appearances for Brazil, is aiming to feature at a fourth World Cup.

Meanwhile, the FIFA World Cup 2026 begins this Friday and Brazil will play their opening group match against Morocco this Sunday at 10am.

The side will then face Haiti next Saturday at 12.30pm before meeting Scotland the following Thursday at 10am.

item.title: Sydney sell-out: Wallabies to kick off 2026 with sold out Ireland Test
Malachi Wrampling's debut Blues season ends in suspension
item.description: Malachi Wrampling’s debut season in Super Rugby Pacific has ended with a suspension, and that’s not the end of the bad news for th

item.title: Somali referee dropped from 2026 FIFA World Cup after US entry denial
item.description: Somalian referee Omar Artan, who was set to become the first Somali official to referee at a FIFA World Cup, has been dropped from the list of match officials after being denied entry to the United States. Artan, who was named the 2025 Confederation of African Football Men’s Referee of the Year, was barred from entering the country at Miami International Airport and is currently in Turkey.
item.content:encoded:
Somalian referee Omar Artan, who was set to become the first Somali official to referee at a FIFA World Cup, has been dropped from the list of match officials after being denied entry to the United States.

Artan, who was named the 2025 Confederation of African Football Men’s Referee of the Year, was barred from entering the country at Miami International Airport and is currently in Turkey.

No reason has been publicly given by US immigration authorities.

However, Somalia is among a number of countries included in a travel ban introduced by US President Donald Trump's administration.

FIFA has confirmed that Artan will be unable to officiate at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

In a statement, FIFA says it is not involved in host country immigration processes, including visa adjudications, and has been informed by authorities that Artan's status will not be changed at present.

The world football governing body says that, as with previous FIFA events, the host government ultimately determines who receives a visa and who is admitted to the country.

The Somali Football Federation has reportedly contacted FIFA seeking urgent clarification on the matter.

Artan became a FIFA referee in 2018 and has previously officiated at the Africa Cup of Nations.

He was among the 52 referees selected by FIFA to officiate at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which will be hosted in Canada, Mexico and the United States from this Friday until 20th July.

item.title: Springboks add Buthelezi and Sadie ahead of international season
item.description: The Springboks have boosted their squad with the inclusion of loose forward Phepsi Buthelezi and prop Carlu Sadie ahead of their international season. Buthelezi, who plays for the Sharks and made his Test debut against Portugal in 2024, will join the squad in Johannesburg, while France-based prop Sadie is expected to arrive on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded:
The Springboks have boosted their squad with the inclusion of loose forward Phepsi Buthelezi and prop Carlu Sadie ahead of their international season.

Buthelezi, who plays for the Sharks and made his Test debut against Portugal in 2024, will join the squad in Johannesburg, while France-based prop Sadie is expected to arrive on Wednesday.

The Springboks have also welcomed back players from the Stormers following their return from Ireland after their United Rugby Championship semifinal.

South Africa began on-field preparations on Monday ahead of their season-opening matches against the Barbarians and Zimbabwe A at Nelson Mandela Bay Stadium in Gqeberha on June 20th.

Springbok coach Rassie Erasmus says there is a real sense of excitement within the squad as they prepare for the new season.

He believes the new additions will settle into the group quickly.

The Springboks are expected to name their squad on June 21st for the opening Nations Championship matches against England, Scotland and Wales.

item.title: Jai Narayan College makes history participating in Vodafone Deans this year
item.description: Jai Narayan College is making history this year as it prepares for its first-ever appearance in the Fiji Secondary Schools Vodafone Deans competition. This was shared by Head Coach for the Under-17 grade Josua Moko to the media during the launching at Gospel.
item.content:encoded:
Jai Narayan College is making history this year as it prepares for its first-ever appearance in the Fiji Secondary Schools Vodafone Deans competition.

This was shared by Head Coach for the Under-17 grade Josua Moko to the media during the launching at Gospel.

Moko says the Deans competition provides a valuable pathway for young athletes across Fiji, creating possibilities not only for boys but also for girls who aspire to pursue rugby at higher levels.

Moko stressed that taking part in the Southern Zone is an investment in helping students grow both on and off the field while exposing them to opportunities that could shape their futures.

He says this prepares the players hollistically.

Moko says it's an ideal time to represent their school and possibly secure a contract overseas and represent their country

item.description: The regular season is over, and now the spotlight shines on four New Zealand giants standing tall in the Super Rugby Pacific semif

item.title: Drova to play in his first Fiji FACT for hometown Navua
item.description: After spending 15 years with Rewa FC and winning some of Fiji football's biggest honours, veteran defender Peniame Drova is prepar
